extra.reason: Shipped in 1.0.0: base-error v5 adopted, voValidated + @shirudo/ddd-kit/http presenter added, two-error-style axis documented across LLM.md/README/CHANGELOG/value-objects guide. Merged to main; version bumped rc.9 -> 1.0.0.
Fixed: handler invocation wrapped in async closure so sync throws become rejections; covered by two new tests
Fixed: deepFreeze treats ArrayBuffer views as atomic (pass-through, unfrozen); 6 new tests; documented limitation
Fixed: cloneBuiltIn passes Promise/WeakMap/WeakSet through by reference; 7 new tests across deep-omit and deep-equal-except
Fixed: cloneBuiltIn aliases Error/ArrayBuffer/SharedArrayBuffer (all reference-compared types); 5 new tests
Fixed: post-commit publish failures caught; committed result always returned; observer-only onPublishError hook added; 5 new tests
Fixed: mutator shadowing for Date/Map/Set + recursive Map/Set content freeze + defensive occurredAt copy; 9 new tests
Fixed: ValueObject deep-clones props via deepOmit before freeze; Entity shallow-copies plain-object/array state before freezeShallow; 5 new tests
Fixed: Object.hasOwn guard in dispatchAndCommit; 6 new tests covering toString/constructor/__proto__/hasOwnProperty/valueOf + loadFromHistory
Fixed: pre-call state snapshot + rollback in catch; 2 new tests (DomainError and non-domain propagation)
Fixed per B+C design: fail-fast plain-data check + toSnapshotState/fromSnapshotState hooks + TSnapshotState generic; 6 new tests; guide + LLM.md updated
Fixed: internal-slot brand probes in isBuiltInObject + Array.isArray routing in deepEqual/deepOmit; 11 new tests
Fixed: ancestor-only cache in path-sensitive mode; 3 new tests (per-path predicate, cycle termination, dedup regression guard)
Fixed: cloneForVo walks plain structures preserving symbol keys; vacuous test made strict; 4 tests
Fixed on fix/p1-review-fixes; covered by 12 new tests
Fixed on fix/p1-review-fixes; covered by 12 new tests

Fixed: brand-verified guard, Error + symbol-key fail-fast, structuredClone-aligned walk; 5 new tests
Fixed: brand-verified guard, Error + symbol-key fail-fast, structuredClone-aligned walk; 5 new tests

Done: shared REFERENCE_COMPARED_TAGS, entity contract docs public. Walker unification deliberately NOT done: cloneForVo (ownership clone) and deepOmit (comparison tool with aliasing semantics) now have documented, intentionally divergent leaf semantics — unifying them would couple two different contracts
Implemented changedKeys/hasChanges dirty tracking on AggregateRoot with docs and 23 new tests; all gates green
design-decisions.md section rewritten: TransactionScope stays minimal, change detection on the aggregate, orchestration in withCommit, opt-in UoW facade as planned next layer
Decided and recorded in design fields; Phase 1 (ddd-kit-ts-rdt) design updated with the resulting API surface
Decided and recorded in design fields; Phase 1 (ddd-kit-ts-rdt) design updated with the resulting API surface

Explicit-save UnitOfWork shipped: run() facade over withCommit, RepositoryFactories registry, UnitOfWorkSession enrollment (saved/deleted, idempotent, save-after-delete throws), error taxonomy (NestedUnitOfWorkError/TransactionClosedError/CommitError/RollbackError/AggregateDeletedError), context invalidation after close, 20 tests, guide + LLM.md + CHANGELOG. Design note: nesting guard is per-instance single-flight; instances are per-operation (parallel-safe by construction).

IdentityMap shipped: class-keyed two-level storage, strict set (no-op same instance, throws on hydrated-twice violation), deletion tombstones (AggregateDeletedError on re-registration), per-run lifetime with clear-on-close, session.identityMap with TransactionClosedError guard. 13 new tests (9 standalone + 4 UoW integration), guide section, repository.md tip, LLM.md, CHANGELOG.

Repository Contract Test Suite shipped as @shirudo/ddd-kit/testing: createRepositoryContractTests(harness), framework-agnostic, 12 contract tests incl. the mandatory two-writer conflict (final state = winner, outbox = winner only), capability-gated version-only/child-collection/cross-instance tests. In-memory reference adapter (real predicate, identity-map read path, enroll-before-write) passes the suite; a mutant test pins that a predicate-less repo FAILS it. Contract refinement: save() enrolls before the row write so the deleted-gate fires as AggregateDeletedError pre-SQL. Guide section 'Proving the contract' as the compliance bar; real-DB requirement for SQL adapters documented. Release gate for the stable claim is satisfied at the kit level.

aggregateVersion shipped as optional DomainEvent field, distinct from event.version (schema). Decision: stamped by withCommit at harvest onto frozen copies (commit version = OCC row version; pre-set values via CreateDomainEventOptions win; pendingEvents untouched; Outbox port unchanged — fully additive). Per-commit granularity documented (ES stream positions stay the event store's concern). Contract suite mandatory test asserts committed outbox events carry it; outbox guide recommends indexed columns. 4 new withCommit tests.

DECIDED: ship it. DuplicateAggregateError (InfrastructureError, pinned name, not retryable, user-safe message without id leak) — same delegation model as ConcurrencyConflictError: kit ships class + per-driver mapping docs (PG 23505, MySQL 1062, SQLite SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_UNIQUE), consumer repo throws on the insert path. Repository guide save() skeleton shows the try/catch mapping; contract suite gains a capability-gated duplicate-insert test (existing row untouched) + mutant test; in-memory reference implements the check; LLM.md stale 'only concrete classes' line fixed. 6 new tests.
Done on feat/uow-abortsignal: contract-suite-mandatory danger callout + cancellation section + scope-sig sync + 3 correctness fixes (uer); AbortSignal pre-flight/context/scope-forward + 6 tests (0e9)
Done on feat/uow-abortsignal: contract-suite-mandatory danger callout + cancellation section + scope-sig sync + 3 correctness fixes (uer); AbortSignal pre-flight/context/scope-forward + 6 tests (0e9)

EventHarvestError (BaseError-direct, non-retryable) for harvest-guard violations; UoW passes it through via cause-chain walk (wrapping-scope safe); CommitError narrowed to transient outbox/commit; code-review fixes folded in; 5 tests

UnenrolledChangesError end-of-run guard via precise load-vs-commit pending-count delta in IdentityMap; best-effort net (contract suite = full mitigation); code-review cleanups (private iteration, shared duck-type helper, best-effort doc); 6 tests

RetryingTransactionScope: someChainRetryable classification, exp backoff +/-20% jitter, last-error-unchanged on exhaustion, AbortSignal-bounded waits, constructor validation; outbox-must-be-transactional documented; shared abortReason helper extracted; code-review fixes folded in; 13 tests
Upgraded @shirudo/base-error 5->7.1.1: self-contained toProblemDetails, dropped removed user-message API, docs+changelog updated. Gates green.
Shipped toPublicErrorView in @shirudo/ddd-kit/presentation (Option B, headless: transport stays with the consumer). 5 new tests, gates green.
Built A: generic Result<T,E> error channel for CommandBus/QueryBus (default string, errorMapper for typed E). Aligns impl with the documented contract. 711 tests green.
Converted AggregateNotFound/Duplicate/ConcurrencyConflict ctors to options objects; removed redundant createDomainEventWithMetadata. All call sites/tests/docs/CHANGELOG updated. 711 tests green.
Implemented DomainStateMachine API with tests and documentation
Hardened DomainStateMachine snapshots, outputs, and wrapper definition ownership
Prepared 2.1.0 release metadata and verified typecheck, tests, lint, build, docs build, and npm pack dry-run.
Clarified the DomainStateMachine guide with a correct AggregateRoot usage example and explicit output handling.
Documented that DomainStateMachine outputs are plain returned values, not emitted Domain Events/EventBus messages; aggregate events still go through recordEvent/commit.
Fixed DomainStateMachine transition lookup to require own transition keys and added regression coverage for Object.prototype event names.

Preserved explicit null/undefined context updates, copied definitions through null-prototype maps so own __proto__ keys survive, and validated malformed runtime definitions as structured errors. Covered by regression tests.
Fixed malformed input and reducer result edge cases with tests
Documented how to model forbidden, guarded, and terminal domain state machine transitions
Copied and deep-froze state machine contexts, aligned constructor snapshot overloads, and documented all structured errors
Fixed pure function snapshot isolation, hardened context clone contract, added edge-case coverage, and updated docs.
Rejected accessor context data, deep-copied/deep-froze transition outputs, added tests, and documented the data-only boundary.
Copied and deep-froze events before guards/reducers, rejected invalid event data, added output invalid-data coverage, and updated docs.
Fixed transition lookup to preserve forbidden-transition semantics before event payload cloning; covered missing and terminal transitions.
Strict guard result validation, terminal definition validation, plain-data rejection for custom class instances, tests, and docs added.
Rejected accessor runtime snapshots and transition result wrappers via descriptor validation; added regression tests.
Rejected descriptor accessors across definitions and array data without invoking getters; rejected Map/Set subclasses; added regression tests.
Rejected non-pure built-ins in domain state machine data and covered edge cases
Fixed all review findings with regression coverage; full tests, typecheck, build, prepublish, and docs build pass.
Closed snapshot invariant, definition-copy, cross-realm data, documentation, and regression-test gaps
Prevented toStringTag accessor execution, added recursive readonly API contracts and type tests, and reused prepared wrapper definitions/results; all release and docs gates pass.

Rejected inherited toStringTag accessors without invocation across realms and arrays, made deepFreeze tag detection accessor-safe, and eliminated redundant wrapper context cloning with deterministic identity tests; all release/docs gates pass.
Fixed internal-slot mutator blocking for Date, Map, and Set with own or inherited toStringTag accessors; added regression coverage and passed release gates.
Added descriptor-aware internal-slot fallback for Date, Map, and Set; deepFreeze and vo now handle own/inherited data toStringTag overrides with regression coverage and all release gates passing.

Generalized accessor-safe built-in classification across supported atomic types, rejected masked Promise/Weak collections, added BigInt value semantics, and covered own/inherited tags plus cross-realm behavior; all release gates pass.
Rejected unsafe custom class and built-in subclass cloning in vo()/ValueObject; added adversarial, cross-realm, and null-prototype coverage; updated docs.
Docs scripts now invoke local TypeDoc and VitePress binaries directly; npm run docs:build passes without nested pnpm policy checks.
Value equality is now fail-closed: object Map keys and Set members are rejected with explicit errors and regression coverage.
VO cloning now uses data descriptors, rejects accessors without invocation, preserves array/symbol/sparse structure, compares array own properties, and bypasses overridden collection iterators.
DomainStateMachine now uses native intrinsic constructor verification for cross-Realm Object/Array prototypes with adversarial context/event/output tests.
Replaced native-code substring detection with captured intrinsic source verification; covered VO and state-machine Proxy spoofing without trap execution.
Preserved sparse arrays and all filtered own properties in deepOmit; covered deepEqualExcept regressions.
Documented the portable Proxy-free trusted-input precondition for DomainStateMachine and Value Objects without claiming an impossible ECMAScript sandbox guarantee.
deepOmit now restores the complete Array length descriptor after cloning entries; covered non-writable length.
Added deterministic depth, unique-node, and own-property budgets across context/event/output cloning with regression coverage.
Depth is now validated before memoized shared references are resolved; property-order bypass covered by regression test.
Added exact-maximum acceptance tests for depth, unique-node, and own-property budgets; temporary >= mutation proved all three tests detect off-by-one regressions.
Split the domain state machine into focused internal modules, preserved the public API, and verified all tests, types, lint, and build.
Completed the state machine guide, converted the executable saga example to the real machine API, added API TSDoc, and verified generated API and VitePress builds.
Added and verified the DomainStateMachine versus XState decision guide.
Replaced the prose comparison with a verified capability and fit table plus concise decision rule.
Clarified that XState can model the same lifecycles and that the kit machine's value is its narrower DDD contract and runtime boundaries.
Renamed the complete public and internal machine input contract, errors, callbacks, tests, examples, changelog, and guide; all tests, types, builds, and generated docs pass.
Added typed DomainError guard rejections with can/dispatch split semantics, exact error propagation, malformed-result validation, atomicity tests, docs, changelog, and generated API verification.
Added typed state-local context invariants with strict runtime validation, documentation, and edge-case coverage.
Implemented a typed, deterministic, side-effect-free definition analyzer with sound graph diagnostics, frozen transition matrices, adversarial tests, and documented proof boundaries.
Documented versioned snapshot migration, transactional inbox/outbox deduplication with optimistic concurrency, explicit deadline inputs, and output versus Domain Event boundaries.

Final release audit completed: no unresolved major architecture findings; 881 tests, lint, typecheck, package build, Typedoc, VitePress, package contents, runtime exports, declarations, naming, docs, and release notes verified.
Completed all DomainStateMachine DDD contract improvements, analyzer, production guidance, tests, and final release audit.
Enforced immutable value semantics for all admitted VO values; added regression tests, docs, and source-state typing fix.

Fixed: constructor now resolves the overload by value, treating explicit undefined snapshot as no-snapshot (falls back to initial). Widened reconstitution overload to accept undefined. Added regression test + inverted the stale type-level assertion.
Fixed with regression tests; typecheck + 900 tests + build green
Fixed with regression tests; typecheck + 900 tests + build green
Fixed with regression tests; typecheck + 900 tests + build green
Fixed with regression tests; typecheck + 904 tests + build green
Fixed with regression tests; typecheck + 904 tests + build green
Fixed with regression tests; typecheck + 904 tests + build green
Implemented onPersistError observer + extracted classifyRunError; tests + typecheck + 908 tests + build green
Implemented onPersistError observer + extracted classifyRunError; tests + typecheck + 908 tests + build green
Fixed via reportToObserver helper for both observers; regression test proven to fail without fix; 909 tests + typecheck + build green
Fixed on branch fix/audit-p1 with TDD (red-green verified); full suite 914/914, typecheck/build/lint clean
Fixed on branch fix/audit-p1 with TDD (red-green verified); full suite 914/914, typecheck/build/lint clean
Fixed on branch fix/audit-p1 with TDD (red-green verified); full suite 914/914, typecheck/build/lint clean
JSDoc guidance in domain-event.ts and handler.ts aligned with the outbox guide (eventId dedup, per-commit watermark semantics); v3 per-event sequence evaluation split into a follow-up bead

Decision: 2.x ships deprecation of the implicit default (JSDoc on setState/autoVersionBump with lost-update mechanics, aggregates-guide warning box, CHANGELOG Deprecated entry); v3 makes bumpVersion a required argument (compile-time forcing beats a silent default flip). v3 work split into follow-up bead.

Decision: opt-in deepFreezeState on EntityConfig/AggregateConfig (option b, explicit config instead of NODE_ENV magic: the kit is edge-runtime-first and env detection is unreliable there). Implemented across constructor/setState/apply/snapshot-restore paths via a protected freezeState method, TDD (8 tests), documented as plain-data-only with in-place ownership transfer of nested inputs. Default stays shallow.

loadFromHistory and restoreFromSnapshotWithEvents now guard the replay target (new UnreplayableAggregateError, crash-loud BaseError posture); the blessing JSDoc example removed, guide corrected; catch-up on persisted aggregates stays supported and is now pinned by tests. TDD, 927/927.

snapshotAt now reads the swappable clock (withClockFactory pins it); clock moved to internal src/aggregate/clock.ts so createSnapshot shares it with createDomainEvent without new public API. TDD, 927/927.

onRetry now routed through the shared reportToObserver helper (extracted from handler.ts into internal src/utils/observer.ts, not exported from barrels); throwing and async-rejecting observers neither stop retries nor mask the original error. TDD (2 RED-verified tests + async pin), 930/930.

All five items: Node claim aligned to 20+ in README/getting-started/LLM.md/edge-runtimes guide; EntityBase drift fixed and both entity.ts JSDoc examples rewritten to setState; commit() naming rationale recorded in design-decisions.md; VO which-API-when section already existed in value-objects.md (covers vo/ValueObject/voWithValidation/voValidated), no change needed; find() pagination warning already linked in IQueryableRepository JSDoc.

Shipped: EventStore<Evt> port (expectedVersion-guarded atomic append, readStream with fromVersion), InMemoryEventStore reference impl (9 TDD tests), and createEsRepositoryContractTests in the testing entry (7 suite tests: mandatory two-writer append conflict, replay equality/fold order, null-for-unknown-stream, identity map, rollback purity + retried first save, fromVersion slicing, capability-gated duplicate-create). Shared contract assertions extracted for both suites; in-repo reference adapter with mutant pinning (blind append, reversed read). Guide section, README bullet, CHANGELOG. 950/950.

AggregateSnapshot carries schemaVersion (stamped from overridable snapshotSchemaVersion, default 1); both restore paths check before assignment: mismatch throws the new SnapshotSchemaMismatchError (InfrastructureError, discard-and-refold guidance) or routes through the overridable migrateSnapshotState hook; legacy snapshots without the field are treated as schema 1 (pinned by test); fields readonly. TDD, 962/962.

getPending ordering (commit order) is now part of the Outbox port contract with SQL guidance (monotonic position + ORDER BY), pinned by test on InMemoryOutbox. New DispatchTrackingOutbox extension (markFailed + deadLetters, attempts on OutboxRecord); InMemoryOutbox implements it with maxDeliveryAttempts (default 5), dead-lettered records leave getPending, markDispatched clears them too; outbox guide documents the retry-then-dead-letter dispatcher loop. TDD, 962/962.

2.x: named UnregisteredHandlerError (busKind, messageType; BaseError crash-loud family) delivered through the error channel with a byte-identical default string message; executeUnsafe throws it. CQRS guide documents the posture. v3 throw split into ddd-kit-ts-efo8. TDD, gates green.

prepareDomainMachineDefinition + PreparedDomainMachineDefinition shipped; the three pure functions and the DomainStateMachine constructor recognize prepared handles via a WeakSet registry and skip per-call validate-and-copy; raw path keeps documented per-call safety. The no-guard input-clone skip was deliberately NOT done: it would change the documented can() semantics (invalid payload must still throw). Guide section added. TDD, gates green.

All entry points (index, utils, testing; http/presentation were already named) use curated named exports; d.ts surface verified byte-identical against the previous build (143/7/10/3/3 names, zero missing/added); new src/api-surface.test.ts pins the runtime export list of every entry. Shipped internals (computeBackoffDelay, freezeShallow) kept until v3 per bead.

v3: bumpVersion is a required argument on AggregateRoot.setState (never-overload keeps Entity base compatibility, TypeError runtime guard fires before mutation for compiler-bypassing calls); autoVersionBump removed, AggregateConfig is now an EntityConfig alias. TDD (type-level RED via unused ts-expect-error, runtime guard pinned), guides + CHANGELOG migration notes. 980/980.

v3: execute throws UnregisteredHandlerError on both buses; errorMapper only sees failures a registered handler produced (pinned: mapper not called). JSDoc, CQRS guide and CHANGELOG migration notes updated. TDD, 980/980.

computeBackoffDelay removed from the package entry (module export stays for its direct-source unit tests); Key/PathSegment hard-renamed to DeepOmitKey/DeepOmitPathSegment per maintainer decision (no aliases). RED via the api-surface guard; d.ts surface diff verified to be exactly the intended three changes. CHANGELOG Removed (breaking) with migration note. 983/983.

One delete contract: IRepository.delete(aggregate), IUnitOfWorkRepository is now the documented minimal subset (delta only exists()). Design decisions recorded: no interface inheritance (generic Evt mismatch not worth the coupling), withCommit-only flow documented (aggregates + deleted arrays), id-only bulk cleanup explicitly excluded from the port (repository-specific purge methods). Type-level RED via ts-expect-error; contract suites unchanged (already aggregate-shaped); repository guide reworked (pattern 2 now uses the deleted marker, pattern 3 load-then-delete + bulk escape). CHANGELOG Changed (breaking) with migration. 983/983.

3.0.0 published to npm (maintainer OTP), tag v3.0.0 and GitHub release live, CHANGELOG dated with the consolidated upgrade checklist, engines Node 22+, base-error peer ^8 (verified empirically against 8.1.0).

v3 epic complete: all five work packages shipped (u9to, efo8, cryw, 8455, zyqs) across PRs 10-12; released as v3.0.0 on 2026-07-05. Out-of-scope decisions honored (no deepFreezeState default flip, commit() name kept, commitSequence parked as needs-driven minor in ddd-kit-ts-yyg7).
Fixed on fix/entity-proto-pollution: loud rejection via HostileStateKeyError, spread-based copy, tests and gates green
Fixed on fix/public-error-view-totality: outer try/catch totality, SCREAMING_SNAKE capability gate, PublicIssue whitelist sanitization; tests and gates green
Fixed on refactor/bus-shared-internals: shared bus-internals module, strict two-directional BusArgs gate, ErrorMapperFailedError containment; review findings applied; gates green
Fixed on refactor/bus-shared-internals: shared bus-internals module, strict two-directional BusArgs gate, ErrorMapperFailedError containment; review findings applied; gates green
Fixed on refactor/bus-shared-internals: shared bus-internals module, strict two-directional BusArgs gate, ErrorMapperFailedError containment; review findings applied; gates green
Fixed on fix/retrying-scope-classifier: classifier guarded like onRetry, original error always surfaces; tests and gates green

Implemented: setState always bumps, setStateWithoutVersionBump for the loud opt-out; apply isNew flag removed; private flag helpers split into named guards; docs, examples, changelog migrated; gates green

Fixed on fix/restore-snapshot-pending-guard: shared non-overridable guard, per-thrower remediation in UnreplayableAggregateError reasons, clean-snapshot undo recipe documented; two review rounds applied; gates green
Fixed on fix/deep-omit-non-enumerable: getOwnPropertyNames walk with enumerability preservation, deepEqualExcept equivalence restored; tests and gates green
Fixed on fix/analyzer-prepared-fastpath: analyzer uses ensureStableDomainMachineDefinition; fast path pinned via passthrough spies; gates green

Lockfile synced to declared ranges (base-error 8.1.0, vitest 3.2.6, critical advisory resolved); kit migrated to base-error 8 public-error API (LocalizedPublicError, ProblemDetails with code member); docs and changelog updated; gates green
Superseded by the StructuredError migration (n1yq): all DSM errors now carry literal codes and name === code, minification-stable by construction

Implemented: all 27 kit errors are StructuredErrors (code = name, SCREAMING_SNAKE), categories DOMAIN/INFRASTRUCTURE/WIRING via shared KitWiringError base, consumer bases keep code+message-only ergonomics, no-base-error path pinned by test, contract matchers re-keyed with legacy aliases, validation gate hardened by category, docs/examples/LLM.md migrated; review round applied; gates green

Implemented version-neutrally: codes are the only accepted identity, no pre-v3 knowledge in code; describeError renders cause-chain names generically; interpretation moved to the CHANGELOG troubleshooting note; gates green
Subsumed by ddd-kit-ts-bva3: extensions can no longer overwrite the RFC 9457 standard members, code, or issues member (reserved-key skip in the merge loop, test-pinned)

Converged onto base-error's toProblem pipeline: thin view-literal delegation, ProblemDetailsResult return (status/headers/body/outcome), issues under details.issues symmetric to toPublicErrorView, member option removed, kit-named ValidationProblemResult alias keeps the no-base-error annotation path; parallel hardening deleted before merge; gates green

Implemented: createKitPublicErrors factory (per-code descriptors, validation family via capability matcher with sanitized details.issues, INTERNAL_ERROR fallback), toPublicErrorView delegates to project/localize with catalog option, honest resolved-locale semantics; factory instead of shared instance because register mutates the underlying catalog; gates green

README imports point at the real @shirudo/ddd-kit/utils entry (node resolve probe verified); internal isBuiltInObject section removed per the utils.ts policy; bv tooling lock ignored
transitionDomainState outcomes are frozen; runtime write via cast throws; test-pinned; gates green
Both dead barrels deleted; ./utils imports resolve unambiguously to src/utils.ts; dist utils surface verified unchanged via node probe; gates green

Subset guard enforced inside the transaction: deleted aggregates missing from aggregates throw EventHarvestError before commit; RED test pins outbox emptiness and preserved pending events; gates green

Adopted (decision revised 2026-07-06: library completeness over demand-waiting): commitSequence stamped next to aggregateVersion at harvest, pre-set-wins, no-copy fast path only when both stamps pre-set; contract suite enforces gapless zero-based sequences; outbox guide teaches the (aggregateVersion, commitSequence) tuple watermark; gates green

Shipped as @shirudo/ddd-kit/money (27 runtime exports). Final scope after six design revisions and four reviews (workflow code review, architecture review, two external responsibility reviews): exact-only money contract (branded via non-exported unique symbol, hard bounds, unknown-taking boundary parsers, moneyFromUnknown re-hydration), lossless arithmetic (add/subtract/negate/rescale), exact-or-rejected parsing (the kit never rounds; rounding is domain policy executed by the consumer's calculation library), structural snapshot bridge, currency-scale resolver seam (Intl resolver = convenience only), Intl formatting. Follow-ups: yvwy (docs sweep), k1vw (tryParse), 866f (property tests), ch6b (optional integration module, decision pending).

Wont-do (user decision, 2026-07-07): library-specificity and a peer dependency are deal breakers. A delegation module cannot avoid naming and depending on a concrete calculation library, and a generic adapter interface would reintroduce the calculator-port speculative abstraction that was already rejected. The snapshot bridge plus the documented domain-named policy pattern (calculateVat(net, vatPolicy) executing via the consumer's calculation library) covers the need with consumer-side one-liners and no kit dependency.

Implemented: IdempotencyStore port, withIdempotentCommit wrapper, InMemoryIdempotencyStore, three structured errors, tests, CHANGELOG. Follow-ups (adapter contract test, guide doc) noted in issue description.

Implemented with one recorded naming deviation from the design note: the poll surface keeps the name Outbox (DispatchTrackingOutbox and all guides build on it) instead of PollableOutbox; semantics exactly as designed. Delivered: OutboxWriter port as the sole write-side dependency of withCommit/UnitOfWork (non-breaking widening); outbox guide sections The kit dispatcher and External dispatchers with a pg-transactional-outbox OutboxWriter adapter example plus CDC and broker-native placement notes and the inbox-overlap note (IdempotencyStore); OutboxDispatcher minimal poller (sequential stop-on-failure preserving per-aggregate causal order, jittered backoff via computeBackoffDelay, markFailed reporting with ack-failure exemption from the poison ceiling, drain mode, AbortSignal graceful stop, observer-based errors); OutboxSink port plus eventBusSink adapter; 9 dispatcher/sink tests; CHANGELOG entry. Found during implementation: DispatchTrackingOutbox with markFailed/deadLetters already existed, so the dispatcher leans on it instead of inventing poisoning logic.

Shipped createOutboxContractTests and createIdempotencyStoreContractTests in @shirudo/ddd-kit/testing, following the repository-contract pattern (harness + environment, capability-gated skips via skippedContractTest, framework-agnostic binding). Outbox suite: commit-order reads, limit paging with stable head, idempotent markDispatched on marked and unknown ids, eventId dedupe, markFailed attempts surfacing, ceiling dead-letters and unblocks successors, no resurrection from late or unknown failure reports, markDispatched clears dead-lettered records, rollback purity gated on providesRolledBackAdds. Idempotency suite: full lifecycle replay with deep-equal outcome round-trip, IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_REUSE on fingerprint mismatch, retryable IDEMPOTENCY_IN_FLIGHT on pending and staged, abandon releases pending and staged but never confirmed, idempotent confirm incl. missing-key no-op, IDEMPOTENCY_COMPLETED_WITHOUT_CLAIM wiring guard, rollback-releases-claim gated on providesRolledBackRuns. Both suites self-tested against the in-memory references (only rollback tests skipped, asserted explicitly). Claiming/SKIP LOCKED deliberately out of scope with a documented note (not portably provable by a generic suite). Also added the production checklist to the outbox guide (same commit scope).

Implemented on feat/event-bus-subscribe-all: subscribeAll(handler) on the EventBus port and EventBusImpl. Catch-all handlers run in the SAME Promise.allSettled batch as the event's typed handlers, so awaited delivery, no-skip-on-peer-failure, error collection after the batch, and input order are unchanged; the batch now also runs when a type has no typed subscriber but a catch-all exists, which strengthens eventBusSink's zero-subscriber story (documented in sink JSDoc and CHANGELOG). Unsubscribe removes exactly one subscription, idempotent. Strict scope held: no predicates, no glob/topic patterns, no onceAll; the strict-sink idea stays parked until a real consumer asks. Six new tests (every-type reception in input order, no-typed-subscriber delivery, shared-batch error collection with AggregateError, twice-subscribed unsubscribe, sync-throw collection, once() unaffected); mock buses in handler/unit-of-work tests extended; breaking-for-hand-rolled-implementations note in CHANGELOG. Gates: 1252 tests, typecheck, build.

Implemented on feat/projection-support: ProjectionCheckpointStore port (load/save per (projection, aggregateId) inside the ambient tx, hasReached position query outside it, reset as rebuild entry), Projector runner (one batch = one TransactionScope tx so update+checkpoint commit and roll back together; stale/duplicate skip via the (aggregateVersion, commitSequence) watermark withCommit stamps; position extractor option for event-store replays; uncursored events reject before anything applies; hasProcessed full-pair wait-for-version, version-only would lie mid-commit; reset runs projection.truncate plus checkpoint reset in one tx; toOutboxSink plugs into OutboxDispatcher), InMemoryProjectionCheckpointStore reference, createProjectionCheckpointStoreContractTests suite (rollback capability-gated), 11 projector tests incl. transactional-fake atomicity proof and dispatcher integration, projections guide rewritten around the shipped runner, CHANGELOG entry, api-surface pinned. Explicit non-goals held: no read-model storage abstraction, no replay source, no privacy handling (documented).

Implemented on feat/snapshot-store: SnapshotStore port (load/save/delete per (aggregateType, aggregateId), latest-only, deliberately transaction-free because snapshots are derived data written after the commit; policy stays consumer-side), InMemorySnapshotStore reference (structuredClone both directions, NUL-separated keys), createSnapshotStoreContractTests (8 tests: round-trip incl. snapshotAt millisecond fidelity and schemaVersion absence, latest-wins, type/id isolation, delete semantics, detached copies both directions; zero capability gates, self-test asserts that), guide rewrite of the snapshot section with the full load path (refold-first Err handling, SnapshotSchemaMismatchError fallback, erasure ordering, out-of-band save policy) plus fix of the stale readSince reference, exports pinned, CHANGELOG entry. Review #9 findings all addressed (see notes).

Implemented on feat/specification: Specification<T> abstract class (isSatisfiedBy, short-circuiting and/or/not combinators with derived ubiquitous-language names, toString = name) plus the specification(name, predicate) factory for flat criteria (blank names rejected loudly, the name IS the adapter contract). Combinator nodes expose their boolean structure via the readonly composite field so storage adapters can walk to named leaves and translate each explicitly; deliberately no expression trees and no translation machinery, per the bead. In-memory evaluation makes findSatisfying a one-line filter for test fakes and in-memory repositories. IQueryableRepository JSDoc rewritten: the old blanket refusal targeted method-less phantom interfaces, the shipped Specification carries evaluation semantics and is a sanctioned TFilter. Repository guide gains a Specifications section incl. the dual-use drift trap (predicate vs translation) and the shared-fixture antidote, and the boundary to projections/intent-revealing methods. 12 tests incl. the documented recursive translation walk. Exports pinned, CHANGELOG entry.

Evaluated: NOT building a saga primitive; the bead's gate criteria fail. (1) Consumer need: not demonstrated; the epic itself arose from the gap sweep, and pattern-completeness is the explicitly disallowed motive. No consumer demand surfaced, including the OmakaseHub live test. (2) Kit-shaped minimal slice: collapses under inspection. A SagaStore port would duplicate IRepository, since saga-as-aggregate over a plain repository with DomainStateMachine inside is the already-proven path (examples/saga runs it end to end with compensation); the correlation helper is a consumer one-liner over event metadata (correlationId exists on EventMetadata); the ONE genuine gap, durable timeouts, is not saga-specific and would be mislabeled as a saga slice; it is parked as a general DeadlineStore evaluation in the backlog (see the new bead). (3) Scope discipline: the 'if built' list (step classification compensatable/pivot/retryable) is the on-ramp to a workflow DSL; the cheapest way to hold the no-DSL line is not to start. Residue: a P3 guide-page bead (compose subscribeAll + withIdempotentCommit as inbox + outbox dispatch + timeout-as-input around the existing example) and the P4 DeadlineStore evaluation. saga-design.md stays consumer-implemented, which is consistent with the kit's stance that orchestration policy is domain code.

Implemented on feat/deadline-store: DeadlineStore port (schedule/cancel joining the write transaction, due/markDelivered/markFailed/deadLetters as the poll surface with the clock passed in by the loop), InMemoryDeadlineStore reference (structuredClone payload detachment, NUL-separated addresses, attempt ceiling), createDeadlineStoreContractTests (13 entries: due-time boundary and millisecond fidelity, earliest-first ordering with up-to-limit paging, idempotent delivery acks by membership, cancel semantics, scope isolation, reschedule-replaces with stale-ack-cannot-consume-successor as the race test, address freed after delivery, attempts/ceiling/dead-letter without cross-address blocking, late-report no-ops plus dead-letter clearing by ack, and both rollback tests gated on providesRolledBackRuns: a rolled-back schedule is a ghost input, a rolled-back cancel must not have removed the wait). Design decisions settled per the bead's open questions: (scope, key) addressing with one pending deadline per address, reschedule = upsert with a fresh incarnation/deliveryId, due returns payload + dueAt + attempts, dead-lettering mirrors the tracking outbox and is part of the single port since deadlines have no head-of-line ordering to protect. Scope limits held: no recurrence, no cron abstraction, no execution engine; guide page docs/guide/deadlines.md shows the drainOnce-style loop and the transactional wiring. Exports pinned, CHANGELOG entry, sidebar registered.

Shipped docs/guide/sagas.md on branch docs/saga-guide, written to the human-prose standard. Content per the bead: the saga as a regular aggregate (DomainStateMachine core, IRepository persistence, OCC as the race arbiter between competing reactions), event intake through the dispatcher-fed bus with subscribe/subscribeAll, correlation as a lookup rather than machinery, withIdempotentCommit as the inbox with the event id as key (full snippet committing saga state, outbox, deadline, and inbox claim in one transaction), commands out through the outbox with the crash window of subscriber-direct dispatch named explicitly (upgrading the example's pattern rather than repeating it), timeouts as inputs via DeadlineStore/DeadlineProcessor including the delivered-deadline-is-a-proposal discipline that the state machine grants for free, compensation as plain business logic pointing to saga-design.md's step classification, and the deliberate non-goals (no SagaStore, no correlation machinery, no workflow DSL). Sidebar registered, CHANGELOG entry, snippets fact-checked against the shipped signatures (withIdempotentCommit deps/request shape, the example's actual AggregateRoot generics).

Evaluated: wontfix, all three gate criteria fail. (1) No demonstrated consumer pain: no consumer codebase has reported forgotten-save production bugs that tests missed; the criterion explicitly excludes pattern-completeness, and Fowler-parity is the only argument on the table. (2) The existing mitigations have proven sufficient and have GROWN since the bead was written: hasChanges makes redundant saves free, enrollment moved the footgun from call sites to repositories, and UnenrolledChangesError now makes the forgotten-enrollment class fail loudly at commit instead of silently dropping writes; the explicit-save stance is a documented design position, not an accident. (3) The magic cost remains unaccepted: auto-flush hides WHEN writes happen and would import exactly the metadata/ordering machinery (CommitOrderCalculator-style) the epic's own MikroORM analysis filed under REJECT. The kit's whole 2026-07 review history doubled down on explicit-over-hidden and loud-over-silent; auto-flush points the other way. MikroORM blueprint notes stay preserved in this bead should a real consumer case ever reopen it.

Evaluated: wontfix, the criteria fail on inspection of what already exists. (1) No consumer use case presented, and the kit's architecture answers read paths without new machinery: projections/read models for query shapes, write-side getById inside a normal run() for read-for-decision. (2) The one scenario the bead names as possibly justifying it, identity-map consistency across several loads in one operation, is ALREADY served: the unit-of-work guide explicitly sanctions read-only run() calls ('Read-only run() calls are fine when several reads need the same...'), a run() that never calls save commits nothing and harvests nothing. What a dedicated read mode would add is only compile-time save-prevention and skipping the write transaction, which is API surface (read-only registry typing, a second run mode, runtime throws) without a driving need, failing criterion (3) under the minimal-surface rule. The §17 sketch stays preserved here if a concrete case ever materializes.

Epic complete, 10/10: all substantive phases shipped in June (explicit-save UnitOfWork core, per-UoW IdentityMap with the deleted-aggregate guard, error taxonomy, all four design decisions, aggregateVersion as a first-class field) and the release-gate child hox delivered the Repository Contract Test Suite that this July's work extended into five further suites (outbox, idempotency, projection checkpoints, snapshots, deadlines), so the epic's hard acceptance stance (OCC as a testable repository contract) is not just met but became the kit's house pattern. The two remaining optional children were evaluation gates and closed today as reasoned wontfixes: auto-flush (explicit save is a documented design position; mitigations grew with UnenrolledChangesError; magic cost unaccepted) and read-only mode (a read-only run() already serves the identity-map-consistency case per the guide; a second run mode is surface without need). Deferral to 2026-08-31 obsolete.

Epic complete, 7/7 children resolved: outbox seam with OutboxWriter split and the minimal OutboxDispatcher (.1), projection support with checkpoint port and Projector (.2), Specification primitive with combinators and buildable visitor layer (.3), saga slice evaluated and closed as reasoned wontfix with the guide page and general DeadlineStore as the residues (.4), SnapshotStore port with contract suite (.5), adapter contract suites for outbox and idempotency store, later joined by checkpoint, snapshot, and deadline suites on the same shared foundation (.6), and EventBus.subscribeAll (.7). Everything went through repeated high-effort multi-agent review rounds; the skill's event-driven prescriptions are now either kit-backed or documented composition (sagas, deadlines guide pages). Follow-on work lives in the P4 adapter-layer backlog epic ddd-kit-ts-41gz.

Shipped on test/money-property-tests. Property suite (fast-check, src/money/money.properties.test.ts): parse/render inversion with zero-excess acceptance and non-zero-excess rejection, add/subtract inverse plus commutativity and associativity, negate as involution and additive inverse, lossless rescale up-and-back identity with rendered-value preservation and lossy-downscale rejection, DTO round-trips through JSON, and the snapshot bridge's safe-integer domain pinned from BOTH sides (the property run itself surfaced that boundary: the naive round-trip law failed on |amount| > MAX_SAFE_INTEGER, which is the documented bridge contract, so the law was split into in-domain round-trip plus out-of-domain loud refusal). Rounding properties out of scope as the description states; the acceptance line about divideRounded was stale (no such API exists). Mutation testing (Stryker + vitest runner, pnpm mutate:money scoped to src/money, break threshold 85): first pass 76.47 percent with 93 survivors; the genuine gaps became src/money/edge-guards.test.ts (describeValue truncation paths were entirely untested, moneyEquals scale difference, isPositiveMoney(0), DTO/parse exact length bounds, excess-fraction regex anchors, snapshot scale-source and type guards, Intl resolver canonical-code contract), landing at 89.76 percent with 412 killed; remaining 42 survivors are dominated by behaviorally equivalent mutants (factory/format cache internals unobservable without spying, regex-anchor mutants masked by the Intl try/catch fallback, the unreachable >= after rescaleMoney's early return). Ops notes: pnpm store migrated (CI=true for non-TTY), Stryker sandbox needed ignorePatterns for .claude socket files and an explicit plugins entry under pnpm; a vitest.config.ts now excludes .stryker-tmp so crashed sandbox leftovers cannot silently double the suite. Suite stays fast (money tests ~350ms; full run 1353).

Shipped on feat/money-try-parse: tryParseMoneyInput, tryMoneyFromDto, tryMoneyFromSnapshot in @shirudo/ddd-kit/money, all three parsers per the scope decision (batch call sites hit all three boundaries; a half family would be inconsistent). The voValidated contract, which is the reason this is a kit feature and not a consumer five-liner: only the documented domain rejections become Err (InvalidMoneyError; plus MoneyPrecisionLossError for decimal-string parsing, typed exactly per parser), everything else keeps throwing via a type-guard predicate, so a bug is never silently counted as a bad input row. Ten tests including the honest edge the totality of the parsers forced: even options-level misuse lands in Err because the parsers answer it with InvalidMoneyError, so the rethrow discipline is proven with a hostile getter instead. Money guide gained the throw-or-Result section mirroring result-vs-throw; surface pins updated; CHANGELOG entry.

Swept on docs/money-anti-pattern-sweep. The three files the bead named (value-objects.md, projections.md, cqrs-and-buses.md) were already clean, fixed on the money feature branch. The remaining offenders were sagas.md plus its example (written after the bead: totalCents/amountCents through Order, Payment, CheckoutSaga, spec, and both guide snippets, all now Money) and the two order examples the README links as teaching material: examples/order now takes a caller-supplied zero like the money guide's invoice slice and accumulates with addMoney; examples/order-with-entity-items carries lineTotal as Money on the item entity, reprices through the caller's policy on quantity change, and reduces the total with addMoney (empty order has no total, since Money carries its currency). Line totals arrive precomputed because the kit ships no multiplication on purpose; the examples say so where a reader would ask. Acceptance grep clean: no guide or example outside money.md models money as number or names a cents field.
Entity.state is protected; IEntity exposes identity only; repositories use snapshot mementos; examples/docs expose domain queries or detached immutable DTOs; typecheck/build and 1390-test suite pass.

Implemented source-owned eventful commit cursor: OutboxWriter now accepts EventCommitCandidate, adapters finalize previousEventfulAggregateVersion from durable source state, state-only and ES contract coverage added, docs/API/changelog migrated. Full typecheck/tests/build pass; changes intentionally left uncommitted for user review.
Genesis predecessor is explicit JSON-safe null across types, projector, in-memory source and contract fixtures; SQS example now publishes the complete committed envelope and groups by source id.

Projector and Projection contracts now require a complete per-address feed; docs and broker mappings prohibit event-type filtering for projector feeds and show explicit no-op handling. Regression coverage proves irrelevant events are consumed/checkpointed and later commits remain contiguous.

Projector now treats envelope.source as authoritative and rejects contradictory optional event aggregateId/aggregateType with ForeignEventError before opening the transaction. Tests cover both mismatch axes, batch-prefix atomicity, unchanged checkpoints, and absent optional event addresses.

InMemoryOutbox now keeps a bounded recent-dispatch eventId receipt cache for post-ack idempotency. Once a receipt is evicted, a descending candidate throws EventHarvestError before mutating the source head. Tests prove both paths leave the next genuine commit linked to the latest head.
InMemoryOutbox rejects a same-event downward pending refresh before mutating the record or source head; regression coverage proves the next genuine commit still links to the previous pending head.
CI package smoke now installs the generated tarball and exact peer versions in an isolated consumer, then imports the main and money package exports; the workflow commands pass locally.

Added an explicit JSON-safe IntegrationMessage boundary with mapper, validating encode/decode, defensive freezing, projector adaptation, public exports, structured error, SQS/domain-event docs, and round-trip/malformed/special-value/security tests.

adoptMintedEvent now applies the shared non-enumerable mint brand, freezes, and registers derived copies; a fresh module instance recognizes an address-stamped pending event via the brand. Full suite, typecheck, lint, and build pass.

Implemented bounded RFC 3339 decoding with canonical UTC normalization; producer remains canonical-only. Added interoperability and rejection regressions, docs, and changelog. Full suite: 1437 passed, 5 expected skips; typecheck, lint, and build pass.

InMemoryOutbox now rejects same-eventId candidates from a different qualified aggregate source before mutating pending, dead-letter, dispatched-receipt, or source-cursor state. Retained receipts store source identity; same-source refresh and dedupe remain intact. Added three RED/GREEN regressions and updated port/docs/changelog. Full suite: 1440 passed, 5 expected skips; typecheck, lint, build, docs build pass.

Removed the always-true isGapAwarePosition operand from the projector apply loop; Phase 0's isValidPosition predicate already narrows every cursored position. Direct isContiguousPosition check compiles and all 33 projector tests plus the full 1440-test suite and build pass.
Moved UNKNOWN_CURRENCY after UNENROLLED_CHANGES and before UNMINTED_EVENT, which is the actual lexicographic position. Typecheck, Biome, API surface, and 41 error tests pass.
Pinned pnpm/action-setup to 10.32.1, matching the verified local toolchain. Workflow YAML parses and the previous Actions log identified the missing version as the sole bootstrap failure.
Implemented genesis-safe checkpoint locking, concurrent adapter contract coverage, and production adapter guidance

Implemented qualified StreamKey across the EventStore port, in-memory adapter, ES harnesses, reusable adapter contract, docs, migration guide, and exports. Verified 1,446 tests, typecheck, lint, build, docs build, production audit, and six-entry tarball smoke; left uncommitted for user review.
Canonical AggregateAddress adopted across ports, EventStore adapter contracts expanded, historical changelog restored, and all gates passed.
Implemented StreamReadResult with explicit absence, actual head reporting, adapter contracts, snapshot recovery documentation, and regression coverage

Implemented exhaustive projection handler maps with explicit ignore token, crash-loud own-key dispatch, checkpoint regression coverage, public exports, guide trade-offs, and changelog documentation; verified 1,468 tests plus all release gates
Added inclusive toVersion reads, adapter contract coverage, and point-in-time reconstruction guidance

Implemented exact full-receipt checks at stored watermarks and within batches; contradictory commit size or predecessor now throws ProjectionReceiptViolationError before apply. Covered by regressions and public error/API tests.

InMemoryOutbox now enforces immutable qualified source positions and exact retained receipts before mutation. Portable outbox contracts now prove commit completeness, predecessor linkage, position identity, source isolation, and rollback head purity. Docs and migration guide updated.

Projection inversion diagnostics now ignore only exact same-position receipts already seen in the batch; exact at-least-once redelivery skips while distinct descending positions still reject. Regression pinned.

Architecture blocker #4 completed: late unknown events cannot silently advance the bounded checkpoint proof, full receipts are immutable where provable, exact redelivery remains idempotent, and adapter source-position/predecessor laws are executable.

Added a batch-local eventId receipt map before all InMemoryOutbox mutations. Contradictory source or candidate positions now reject atomically; exact value-semantic repetition remains idempotent. Regression observed red before implementation; full gates pass with 1492 tests and 5 expected skips.

Implemented shared transient/permanent/unknown delivery classification for outbox and deadlines, safe classifier-failure assessments, retryable/timeout defaults, tests, public types, and migration documentation.

Bound outbox/deadline poll and state-store I/O with EffectContext and storageTimeoutMs, documented native cancellation obligations and unknown late-write outcomes, and covered hanging operations, shutdown, ignored cancellation, and late acknowledgements.

Implemented one absolute post-commit observer/bus budget with expired-effect preflight, Projector pre-abort before validation/transaction setup, deterministic timer tests, and migration documentation.
Removed the former boolean countsTowardCeiling option from the v3 API and implementation; classifyFailure is now the only policy hook, with compile-time removal coverage and updated migration docs.
Documented the intentional slow-poison trade-off: shell delivery timeouts retry with backoff indefinitely by default, with classifyFailure as the explicit opposite policy.

Expanded the EventBus guide with a reader-oriented explanation of effects, the imperative shell, shared publication budgets, cooperative cancellation, zombie work, and why AbortSignal stays outside domain events.
